chunks.text: IRIS News | Page 5 | Instructional & Research Information Systems Skip to main content Skip to primary navigation Skip to primary sidebar Since some time last night, our mailing list server lists.eecs has been offline due to an issue with the system.

has been offline due to an issue with the system. Staff are beginning to investigate and an update will be posted here when available. While the list server is offline, some emails will be delayed, and any messages sent through an EECS mailing list

nd any messages sent through an EECS mailing list will not be delivered on time. Please contact help@eecs.berkeley.edu if you have any questions. Update: Dec 12, 9am, Rob McNicholas After installing the most recent patches, this machine would not boo

he most recent patches, this machine would not boot up. We reverted to a previous kernel and the system is now up. Due to the Winter Holiday, Energy Curtailment, and New Year’s Holidays, the Berkeley campus is officially closed for the period Tuesday

campus is officially closed for the period Tuesday, December 24 to Wednesday, January 1, reopening on Thursday, January 2, 2024. Additionally, much of campus is on reduced support for January 2-3. All departmental computing servers will remain on and

departmental computing servers will remain on and should be available during the break, but there will be no staff on site to provide any computing support or handle any requests. Automatic monitoring systems will alert staff to critical infrastruct

g systems will alert staff to critical infrastructure problems, and any such problems will be dealt with on a best-effort basis. You may wish to turn off any workstations or printers in your office, to help with energy savings. The IRIS Helpdesk offi

o help with energy savings. The IRIS Helpdesk office will be closed Monday, December 23 through Friday, January 3, re-opening on Monday, January 6. If you need assistance between January 2-3, please email help@eecs, and we may be able to schedule an

mail help@eecs, and we may be able to schedule an appointment with you. Problem reports can be left on the Helpdesk voice mail (510-642-7777) or emailed to help@eecs during the campus closure period, but staff may not receive or reply to these messag

but staff may not receive or reply to these messages until at least January 2. Requests for network support or new IRIS accounts that are received after noon Tuesday, December 17 may not be processed until January 6, 2024. During the break, Instructi

until January 6, 2024. During the break, Instructional labs will be locked, but the main servers will remain online (for details, see the Instructional website ). Instructional support staff will occasionally monitor inst@eecs email over the break. P

sionally monitor inst@eecs email over the break. Please be aware, though, that staff may not be able to respond or reply to messages until at least January 2. Computing support staff will be monitoring their email when possible, so please use standar

g their email when possible, so please use standard email addresses to report any urgent problems. As a reminder: Instructional Computing Environment: inst@eecs Research / Staff Computing Environment: help@eecs Information regarding campus IT closure

help@eecs Information regarding campus IT closures is available here: https://technology.berkeley.edu/news/reduced-it-support-winter-holidays Happy Holidays! Microsoft will release their patches for the month on Tuesday, December 10th, 2024. Various

the month on Tuesday, December 10th, 2024. Various IRIS Windows and Linux servers will be patched and rebooted Tuesday or Wednesday. PRINT, WINTERM and WDS will automatically patch and reboot Wednesday, December 11th, at 3:00am. WINTERM may be unavai

y, December 11th, at 3:00am. WINTERM may be unavailable until 10:00am Wednesday, depending on additional software updates. Linux servers lists.eecs, iris2.eecs (for access to the network and roster databases), and login.eecs/login-mfa.eecs will be pa

tabases), and login.eecs/login-mfa.eecs will be patched and rebooted Wednesday night. DNS servers cronus, themis, and rhea may also be briefly unavailable while rebooting at approximately 8:00pm Tuesday. On Wednesday, December 11th, the departmental

ay. On Wednesday, December 11th, the departmental DHCP server is scheduled for downtime from 10pm to 10:30pm (actual downtime is expected to be shorter than scheduled) [Read more…] about December IRIS Server Patching Tuesday afternoon and evening, No

Server Patching Tuesday afternoon and evening, November 19, we had a disruption of DNS resolution for the eecs.berkeley.edu domain on the campus nameservers. This meant that many systems outside of the EECS network were temporarily unable to resolve

he EECS network were temporarily unable to resolve *.eecs.berkeley.edu hostnames — so people may have been temporarily unable to reach EECS websites or deliver email to some @eecs.berkeley.edu addresses. [Read more…] about DNS disruption for eecs.ber

es. [Read more…] about DNS disruption for eecs.berkeley.edu, Nov 19 We are scheduling a NetApp ONTAP upgrade, to apply a necessary update to our NetApp fileserver, from 8am to 12pm on Saturday, November 30, 2024 . All Home directories and Project sha

er 30, 2024 . All Home directories and Project shares (CIFS/SMB and NFS) may experience disconnection during the update that may cause data loss on active, open files. Please close your open files and save them before Saturday morning. Services (e.g

d save them before Saturday morning. Services (e.g. webservice) that depend on this storage will also be temporarily down or unresponsive. When maintenance is completed, NFS clients should automatically resume normal operation, but a reboot should he

ly resume normal operation, but a reboot should help if they don’t. CIFS clients (such as Windows and MacOS) may need to remount filesystems, or simply be rebooted. [Read more…] about NetApp Fileserver Maintenance, Saturday November 30, 2024 On Thurs

r Maintenance, Saturday November 30, 2024 On Thursday, November 14th, between 6:30 and 9:45 AM, Cory Hall third floor wired and wireless networks will be offline due to electrical work being performed at the main service panel. Facilities is performi

at the main service panel. Facilities is performing this work in order to restore electrical power to the Cory Hall Helpdesk. Facilities is reporting that the west side of the third floor (which includes the network closet) will be without power fro

udes the network closet) will be without power from 6:30 – 9:30 AM. The network devices, particularly the wi-fi APs, will need some time to boot up after power is restored. We typically expect network connectivity to be restored within 10-20 minutes

connectivity to be restored within 10-20 minutes after power returns to the network closet. Update 11/14 9:36AM: This work was apparently completed without any interruption to the network. Please contact help@eecs.berkeley.edu if you have any questi

tact help@eecs.berkeley.edu if you have any questions. Microsoft will release their patches for the month on Tuesday, November 12th, 2024. Various IRIS Windows and Linux servers will be patched and rebooted Tuesday or Wednesday. PRINT, WINTERM and WD

booted Tuesday or Wednesday. PRINT, WINTERM and WDS will automatically patch and reboot Wednesday, November 13th, at 3:00am. WINTERM may be unavailable until 10:00am Wednesday, depending on additional software updates. Linux servers lists.eecs, iris2

software updates. Linux servers lists.eecs, iris2.eecs (for access to the network and roster databases), and login.eecs/login-mfa.eecs will be patched and rebooted Wednesday night. DNS servers cronus, themis, and rhea may also be briefly unavailable

, themis, and rhea may also be briefly unavailable while rebooting at approximately 8:00pm Tuesday. On Wednesday, November 13th, the departmental DHCP server is scheduled for downtime from 10pm to 10:30pm (actual downtime is expected to be shorter th

30pm (actual downtime is expected to be shorter than scheduled) [Read more…] about November IRIS Server Patching On Thursday, October 31 between 1 and 5PM, we will be performing network link migrations in preparation for retiring an old router. This

s in preparation for retiring an old router. This maintenance will affect one of the EECS network’s paths to Campus and the Internet, however we expect no interruption as we have another redundant path which is able to handle our full traffic volume.

h which is able to handle our full traffic volume. Update 10/31 16:22: This work has been completed without any significant interruption to network traffic. Please contact us at help@eecs.berkeley.edu if you notice any issues. Microsoft will release

if you notice any issues. Microsoft will release their patches for the month on Tuesday, October 8th, 2024. Various IRIS Windows and Linux servers will be patched and rebooted Tuesday or Wednesday. PRINT, WINTERM and WDS will automatically patch and

lists.eecs, iris2.eecs (for access to the network and roster databases), and login.eecs/login-mfa.eecs will be patched and rebooted Wednesday night. DNS servers cronus, themis, and rhea may also be briefly unavailable while rebooting at approximatel

riefly unavailable while rebooting at approximately 8:00pm Tuesday. On Wednesday, October 9th, the departmental DHCP server is scheduled for downtime from 10pm to 10:30pm (actual downtime is expected to be shorter than scheduled) [Read more…] about O

to be shorter than scheduled) [Read more…] about October IRIS Server Patching System administrators in EECS may be familiar with our DNS nameservers cronus and rhea. We now have a new nameserver, physically located in Cory Hall, named themis. If your

ically located in Cory Hall, named themis. If your systems are typical DHCP clients, or you don’t use the EECS wired network, then you should have nothing to change. We are planning to replace and retire rhea according to the following schedule: Thu

ire rhea according to the following schedule: Thu Oct 15 — We will update our DHCP configuration, telling Cory Hall DHCP clients to use themis and cronus, and telling other DHCP clients to use cronus and themis. November/December — We will monitor an

and themis. November/December — We will monitor any continued usage of rhea for DNS, and periodically alert system administrators so they can update their systems to use themis instead. early January — We will retire rhea, but will point its name and

— We will retire rhea, but will point its name and IP address at cronus. « Go to Previous Page Go to page 1 Interim pages omitted … Go to page 3 Go to page 4 Go to page 5 Go to page 6 Go to page 7 Interim pages omitted … Go to page 192 Go to Next Pag
